summary:
Oral contraceptives pose significant health threats primarily through increasing the likelihood of severe blood clots; thus, patient education regarding symptoms should be prioritized for early detection and treatment. This summary captures the essence that thromboembolic issues are critical while also touching upon preventive measures recommended based on identified high-risk factors related to OCPs' adverse effects. To streamline further communication clarity without redundancy,

Final Summary: High risk from oral contraceptives mainly involves clot formation leading to potentially fatal outcomes requiring vigilant symptom monitoring among users. Key focus areas include DVT/Pulmonary Emboli prevention given strong epidemiological links post-ingestion initiation periods especially concerning young smokers/consumers needing close follow-up care plans tailored accordingly per individual profile characteristics discussed previously during consultations.
